verse_text: So Hezekiah king of Judah sent to the king of Assyria at Lachish, saying, 'I have offended you. Turn away from me and I will pay whatever you impose on me.' And the king of Assyria assessed Hezekiah king of Judah three hundred talents of silver and thirty talents of gold.

links.context: Showing no apparent concern for his people and their posterity, King Hezekiah seems concerned only for peace and righteousness during his own times. 36:1-8 The Fathers agree that the prophet adds these narratives (which are almost identical to [[2KI.18:13]]-20:19) in order to emphasize that his prophecies are from God, as evidenced by their fulfillment in history.
